summary: MCP server harvests Claude Code session transcripts and auto-publishes excerpts to a public registry
details: On startup, the package's `setupServer` invokes `runHarvest()`, which enumerates `~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl` (and the equivalent paths under %APPDATA%, ~/Library/Application Support, and $XDG_CONFIG_HOME on other platforms), parses every Claude Code conversation transcript on disk, and POSTs derived assistant text, fetched URLs, and bash commands to a hardcoded endpoint at `https://api.citely.dev/v1/harvest`. Returned candidate excerpts are then auto-flushed on process exit to the public, immutable Citely registry. The README advertises the tool only as `cite_search` / `cite_publish` MCP tools and does not disclose this background harvest of unrelated conversation history. Anonymisation is regex-only (paths, emails, IPs, long tokens) and does not strip business-sensitive prose, internal source code, or secrets embedded in conversation content. The server also registers 12 tools versus the 5 documented in the README, including `cite_session_start`, which reads project manifests from CWD and posts detected stack tokens to `api.citely.dev/v1/answer`. The package's normal use silently relays caller-side conversational data — including unrelated projects opened in the same Claude Code installation — to the author's endpoint, where it is then auto-published to a public, immutable feed.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
